resources.string: Mobile VRChat log capture with a Scarlet-style player log view.

Before Scarlet shows the full mobile dashboard, pair it with Wireless Debugging once.

1. Turn on Wireless debugging in Developer options.
2. Tap "Pair device with pairing code".
3. Enter the 6-digit code below.
4. If your phone also shows a port, paste it into the optional field.

After pairing succeeds, Scarlet switches to the full capture view automatically.

After pairing, Scarlet focuses on the same player log details desktop Scarlet cares about: username, user id, avatar, joined, left, age verification, and pronouns. Turn Wireless Debugging back on if needed, then connect capture.
Optional port from the pairing dialog
Battery optimization: still enabled. Scarlet needs unrestricted battery use to keep logging while VRChat is in the foreground.
Battery optimization: unrestricted battery use granted.
Enter the 6-digit code shown on the phone
Connected. Waiting for VRChat to write a log. If nothing appears, enable full logging in VRChat\'s Debug settings...
Connected. Waiting for VRChat to start writing a log. If nothing appears, enable full logging in VRChat\'s Debug settings...
Allow unrestricted battery use for Scarlet before connecting.
Approve unrestricted battery use for Scarlet, then return here and tap Connect.
Open the battery optimization list, set Scarlet to unrestricted, then return here and tap Connect.
Battery optimization settings are unavailable on this device.
Waiting for live VRChat log lines...
No player entries yet. Once Scarlet sees VRChat join and player events, this screen becomes your mobile player log.
Username | User ID | Avatar | Joined | Left | 18+ | Pronouns
Avatar: waiting for avatar switch log
18+: unavailable from current mobile log source
Pronouns: unavailable from current mobile log source
Keeps the VRChat log tailer alive while the app is running.
